exemplar.title: Investigation on the working memory model by experimenting the impact of background music on memory formation measured by performance in a memory recall test

detailedExemplarData.title: Investigation on the working memory model by experimenting the impact of background music on memory formation measured by performance in a memory recall test

subject.title: Psychology
subject.description:
IB Psychology is a Group 3 subject in the International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP). The course examines human behavior and mental processes through scientific inquiry, exploring biological, cognitive, and sociocultural approaches to understanding behavior. Students develop critical thinking skills while investigating contemporary psychological research, research methods, and ethical considerations in psychological practice.

The course is available at Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours) and Higher Level (HL, 240 hours), with first assessment in 2027.

Assessment consists of Paper 1 (integration of concepts, content, and contexts through short-answer and extended-response questions: SL 2 hours, HL 2 hours, SL 50%, HL 40%), Paper 2 (application of psychological knowledge to research contexts: SL 1 hour, HL 2 hours, SL 25%, HL 20%), Paper 3 (HL only: research methods and data analysis, 1 hour, 20%), and Internal Assessment (research proposal development: SL 25%, HL 20%).

strengths: Clear, focused aim with explicit relevance to educational practice
Accurate description of the working-memory model with a link to dual-task interference
Well-operationalized, directional experimental hypothesis with null hypothesis stated
weaknesses: Terminology occasionally informal (e.g., âcognitive resourcesâ vs âattentional control systemâ)
Brief costâbenefit discussion limits depth of relevance explanation
Could deepen the discussion of prior findings (e.g., Perham & Vizard) to strengthen rationale
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The introduction meets most top-band descriptors: a clear aim, full model description and explicit theoretical link, plus fully operationalized hypotheses. Minor informality in terminology and a concise relevance discussion justify a score of 4 rather than 5 or 6.

strengths: Research design is correctly identified and its rationale (avoiding order effects) is explained
Controlled variables and materials are listed and justified, supporting validity
weaknesses: Opportunity sampling is described but its bias and impact on generalizability are not evaluated
No justification is given for the use of AI-generated story material
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Although the design and materials control are well explained, the exploration lacks critical evaluation of sampling bias and key justifications, placing it solidly in the middle of the 1â2 band (score 2).

strengths: Appropriate use of descriptive (median, SD) and inferential (MannâWhitney U) statistics with correct significance decision
Findings are interpreted clearly and linked to the hypothesis
weaknesses: Use of SD with ordinal data is inappropriate; interquartile range would suit better
No effect-size reported to convey practical significance
Graphical presentation omits error bars and clear unit labels
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The analysis applies correct statistical tests and interprets outcomes with reference to the hypothesis, meriting a top band score in the 3â4 range. Minor calculation conventions and graphical omissions prevent a higher mark.

strengths: Findings are discussed in relation to the working-memory model
Strengths and limitations are identified, explained and tied to validity
Modifications are clearly linked to specific limitations
weaknesses: Discussion is concise and could further explore theoretical implications of a non-significant result
Evaluation would benefit from deeper critical reflection on time-pressure and longitudinal extensions
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Evaluation shows a strong link to theory and justified modifications, but its concise nature and presentation flaws limit its depth, warranting a mid-band score of 3.

strandSelections.reasoning: A bar chart is provided and labelled, addressing the hypothesis by comparing median recall across conditions.  However, axes lack clear units beyond raw score, there are no error bars, and the choice of bar chart for medians is debatable.  These minor technical faults merit placement in the 3-4 band, lower score 3.

The student states the statistical outcome, links it to acceptance of the null and explicitly discusses what this means for the hypothesis and data.  They acknowledge direction and size (no significant difference) and integrate descriptive results.  Interpretation is clear but theoretical implications are only briefly explored here, so a 5 (not 6) is appropriate.

Both hypotheses are clearly written.  The IV (presence vs absence of background classical music) and the DV (number of correct answers out of six) are **fully operationalised** inside the experimental hypothesis.  Directionality and significance level are declared, meeting all top-band requirements without evident flaw; therefore the maximum 6 is justified.

Opportunity sampling is identified; participant characteristics (grade, age, academic background) are given; and the student **explains** convenience as the rationale.  The explanation is limited (no evaluation of bias), positioning the strand at 3 within the 3-4 band.

The working-memory model is **accurately described**, including the phonological loop, visuo-spatial sketchpad, episodic buffer and central executive.  The student **explicitly links** the model to dual-task interference and to the chosen IV (background music) and DV (recall), demonstrating how the investigation tests predictions of the model.  Some terminology (e.g., âcollection of cognitive resourcesâ) is slightly informal, hence a 5 rather than 6.

Findings are **discussed in relation to the working-memory model**, noting the unexpected absence of interference and considering implications for phonological-loop capacity.  The discussion is accurate but relatively concise, warranting 5 rather than 6.

The student states a focused aim (âdetermine the effect of simultaneous use of a phonological loop by 2 auditory stimuli on memorizationâ) and **explicitly explains its relevance** to educational practice and student study habits.  The explanation makes clear why the outcome matters for the identified target population, satisfying the descriptor for the highest band.  Minor brevity in the cost/benefit discussion keeps the quality nearer the lower end of the 5-6 range, so a score of 5 is appropriate.

Multiple strengths and limitations are **explained and directly tied to validity, reliability and generalisability**.  Each point is elaborated (e.g., language proficiency control, opportunity-sampling bias).  Depth is good though evaluation could be more critical; thus a solid 5.

Controlled variables (text, time, environment, language proficiency checks, volume) and materials are listed **and reasons for their control are provided**.  The description goes beyond listing by justifying controls (e.g., equalising difficulty, researcher triangulation), fully meeting âexplainedâ; level of detail merits the upper score of 4 in this band.

Proposed modifications (different music genre, random sampling, pre-test) are **clearly linked to specific limitations** and justified in terms of improving validity or scope.  Justification lacks methodological detail (e.g., how to implement random sampling), so the work earns 5 within the top band.

The student identifies an independent-groups design and **explains why it was chosen** (avoids order effects, time constraints).  The rationale is brief but clear, matching the âexplainedâ descriptor.  Minor omissions (no discussion of between-group variability) keep it at the lower end of the 3-4 band.

Appropriate descriptive (median, SD) and inferential (MannâWhitney U) statistics are chosen for ordinal, non-parametric data.  Calculations appear accurate and decision about significance is correct.  A conceptual slip occurs using SD with ordinal medians, and no effect-size is reportedâminor errors that place work at the top of the 3-4 band rather than 5-6.

annotations.comment: Insightful link between findings and model adaptation, suggesting a potential revision of phonological-loop capacity.
Connect the proposed longitudinal extension explicitly to the identified time-pressure limitation.
The sample limitation description in Table 2 is truncated and needs full elaboration on generalisability issues.
Second limitations table is misaligned and entries are unclear; reformat for clarity.
Include additional peer-reviewed references to strengthen the literature base beyond four sources.
Clarify how the ChatGPT citation influenced material creation and address potential AI-related biases.
The title is concise, clearly linking the working memory model and background music impact, setting a strong foundation for the investigation.
The aim is well articulated, explicitly tying dual auditory tasks to memorization performance, which aligns with the working memory framework.
Describing the central executive as a âcollection of cognitive resourcesâ is informal; refine this using standard terminology (e.g., attentional control system).
Expand the Perham and Vizard (2011) discussion by detailing how their irrelevant sound findings directly support your hypothesis.
Strong justification of target population: linking outcomes to student study habits enhances practical relevance.
Independent variable is operationalized precisely as presence versus absence of classical music during memorization.
Dependent variable is fully operationalized as number correct out of six, ensuring clarity in measurement.
The experimental hypothesis is one-tailed, directional, and clearly linked to working memory interference predictions.
Null hypothesis is succinctly stated with equivalent performance predicted, which aids transparency in statistical testing.
Research design is well chosen and explained, with rationale for independent-groups to avoid order effects and demand characteristics.
Comprehensive materials list includes researcher triangulation and pilot testing, which supports reliability.
Justify use of AI-generated story by discussing its validity and consistency for memorization tasks.
Ethical considerations are thorough, covering informed consent, anonymity, debriefing, and right to withdraw.
Controlled variables are justified in relation to validity and reliability, demonstrating careful experimental control.
Participant sampling lacks discussion of opportunity-sampling bias and its impact on generalisability; evaluate this limitation.
Opportunity sampling is described but lacks evaluation of its convenience versus bias implications.
Consider reporting effect size along with median to convey practical significance of group differences.
Using standard deviation with ordinal data is inappropriate; use interquartile range or range instead.
Add a clear title and label units in Table 1 to improve clarity of median and SD presentation.
The bar chart omits error bars and clear axis units; include variability indicators and unit labels.
Phrasing should be âfail to reject the null hypothesisâ rather than âaccept the null hypothesis.â
Include the Z-score and exact p-value in the main analysis narrative for completeness.
Expand evaluation by discussing how the non-significant finding refines predictions of the working memory model.
